key_points: Nearby off-campus apartments are dirty, mismanaged, and overpriced
5025 and 6nineteen apartments have serious maintenance and billing issues
SDSU takes no responsibility for conditions in affiliated housing
key_quotes: My AC and heat has been broken since early December. 5025 even resolved my maintenance request without entering my apartment.
Don't be lured in by the 'low' cost. Parking: scam. Wifi: slow. Cleanliness when we moved in: garbage.
The community electric charge was really frustrating because it wasn't clearly explained.

key_points: Party school reputation is real — bikini daygers happen regularly on Fridays
Transfers and older students consistently report feeling isolated and friendless
Greek life is divisive; sorority rush described as 'humiliating' and 'phony'
key_quotes: Frat parties/daygers combined with summer weather in February.
I've been going to SDSU since 2024 as a transfer student and I just have not been able to make any friends whatsoever.
The whole process was humiliating, phony filled with lots of insincere girls.

key_points: Campus food described positively in passing, Panda Express mentioned
No sustained complaints about on-campus dining quality
Food trucks and nearby options seem popular with students
key_quotes: Get ready for beautiful campus, great weather, hikes by the beach and surprisingly good food on campus.
I hope you like being fat because we got California burritos and carne asada fries.

key_points: Loneliness and isolation are recurring, serious issues especially for transfers
Strict no-absence policies force sick students to attend class, worsening health
Suicidal ideation mentioned by multiple students; moderators explicitly warn about it
key_quotes: Suicide was definitely something that crossed my mind more than once due to the feeling of loneliness.
I showed up to class with what I thought was a bad cold, but it ended up being Whooping Cough. The professor has a really strict attendance policy.
I am failing majority of my classes. I struggle a lot with mental health and it spiraled severely this semester.

key_points: Disbursement timing causes major anxiety; students fear being dropped from classes
Financial aid process is confusing and stressful for many students
In-state tuition is a genuine selling point compared to UCs
key_quotes: My aid got disbursed it says but my balance is still there and I have to pay by the 20th what do I do?
If I just pay off my tuition now will I just get reimbursed? I want to make sure I'm not dropped from my classes.
I graduated from SDSU in 1989. Back then the CSU system's mission was to make quality education possible for any California resident.

key_points: CS program widely criticized as weak; students regret not transferring elsewhere
Impacted majors like business require pre-major status before full admission
CC-to-SDSU transfer jump is real — students report going from 3.9 GPA to failing
key_quotes: SDSU has a really bad CS department.
I was a 3.9 in CC, now at SDSU I'm getting mostly C's and a couple of F's on midterms. Are CCs really this much easier?
Cancellation of undergrad courses with fewer than 30 enrolled is literally next — this will inevitably make graduating harder.

key_points: Admin approved $10M student fee hike without a student vote
President received near-$500K salary with a 10% raise while fees increased
Library hours cut, course minimums raised — students feel nickel-and-dimed
key_quotes: Don't forget the president of SDSU just got a raise — almost $500k base salary, over 10% raise for doing nothing useful.
SDSU approved $10,000,000 student fee hike without student vote. We ain't getting that money back.
SDSU loves to take everyone's money in any way they can.

key_points: Campus is widely praised as beautiful with great weather year-round
Parking is a genuine nightmare — P12 has weekly hit-and-runs and road rage
Trolley commute has safety concerns; MTS security number shared by students
key_quotes: Get ready for amazing beaches, coffee shops, farmers markets, shopping, restaurants.
P12 is full of trash 24/7 all over the floor and smells. Hit and runs happen about once a week.
Half my Reddit notifications are from P12. Mad Max up in that place.

key_points: Business and nursing programs have strong alumni and employer networks
CS degree viewed skeptically; students advised to go elsewhere for tech careers
Alumni report good outcomes, especially those who networked and did internships
key_quotes: I have a BA and an MA from SDSU and am a 26-year employee at UCLA.
Be proactive. Engage with professors about research and internships — these will set you apart.
SDSU for CS and it's not even close [that SJSU is better].

key_points: In-state tuition is genuinely affordable versus UCs — a real advantage
Fees alone approaching $2,000/semester on top of tuition; students are frustrated
Off-campus housing costs and surprise utility charges erode the savings
key_quotes: Fees alone are almost $2000 for this semester. Is there any way I can opt out?
It amazes me that you have to pay a fee to graduate.
SDSU free vs USC $30k — if I attend SDSU I will not pay a cent for tuition and could stay living at home.

key_points: Strong school spirit, especially around basketball and football
Campus culture leans social and party-forward; academics secondary for many
Warm, supportive community online but real loneliness reported on campus
key_quotes: I walk past so many beautiful people going to class I just feel like giving y'all high fives.
SDSU: I peaked in HS and want to party but am not smart enough for UCSB.
I love this school — good morning and I wish everyone a nice fall semester.

key_points: Administration: $10M fee hike imposed without student vote, president paid $500K
Housing: off-campus apartments have broken AC, hidden utility fees, ignored maintenance requests
Safety: weekly hit-and-runs in P12, road rage incidents, trolley safety concerns reported
key_quotes: They denied my appeal despite my dad having cancer and me wanting to stay close to home. They're heartless money suckers.
My ceiling fan [broken] — management hasn't answered any of my countless emails.
Someone get hit on College and Canyon? A Blue Honda Civic cut across turning lanes into picketers.

key_points: Appeals actually work — one student got in after being told 'appeals never work'
Student-built Chrome extension puts RateMyProfessor ratings directly in class search
Campus has beloved stray cats (named Genie) cared for by students and staff
key_quotes: Everyone kept telling me 'appeals never work.' I still sent one in. And today — I GOT IN.
I built a Chrome extension that automatically puts RMP ratings next to each professor's name on the class search page.
That's Genie. They get fed every day by students and staff. There's a website someone made about the cats on campus.
